reviews.review_text: You shouldn't legally be allowed to release a game this terribly made and charge 60 dollars. this game was already terribly optimized idk what they could have possibly done but somehow they've managed to make it worse. People will defend wild card by saying snail games is the real problem but not wildcard has also always been a problem. it shouldn't soft lock my computer when i turn on console commands fix your ass game

[h1]“6200+ hours… and I’m still here. That should tell you everything.”[/h1]

[b]Mixed[/b]  
6204 hrs on record

[hr]

[h2]🦖 First Things First[/h2]  
I’ve got over 6,200 hours in ARK.

I play on official PVE servers, mainly focusing on breeding—taking dinos down to clean Level 1 lines and selling them to stat and color breeders.

So yeah—I clearly like the game.

But let’s not pretend everything is fine.

[hr]

[h2]⚔️ The Good (Because There IS Good)[/h2]  
When ARK works, it’s one of the best survival games out there.

• Taming dinos never gets old  
• Building bases with your tribe is great  
• Breeding and trading gives the game long-term depth  

There’s nothing quite like it when everything clicks.

[hr]

[h2]💥 The Reality (Official Server Life)[/h2]  
Now let’s talk about what you’re actually signing up for.

• Lag… constant lag  
• Server crashes… a lot of them  
• Bots spamming RMT Discords nonstop (even when they say it’s “fixed”)  

And every time there’s a patch or a new map?

[b]Something breaks.[/b]  
Not “might break.”  
[b]Will break.[/b]

[hr]

[h2]🏗️ Building? Good Luck.[/h2]  
Playing on official PVE?

Hope you like pillars.

• Pillar spam everywhere  
• Entire areas blocked off “just because”  
• Finding a legit base spot can take hours  

Moved from a sunset server?

Yeah… good luck finding space.  
Everything’s already claimed.

[hr]

[h2]🛠️ Updates & Timing[/h2]  
Think something will release on time?

Yeah… no.

You’d have better odds guessing lottery numbers than predicting a correct release window.

[hr]

[h2]🏝️ Official Servers… For Now[/h2]  
Think your official server will always be there?

LOL.

They’ve already started sunsetting “inactive” servers again. This isn’t even the first time.

So if you’re investing time into a server—just understand there’s always a chance it disappears when player counts drop.

[hr]

[h2]🧩 Mods & Stability[/h2]  
Think your mods will just… stay installed and work?

pffff.

Get ready to reinstall them.  
Again.  
…and again.

Because sometimes they just disappear, break, or stop working for no reason after updates.

[hr]

[h2]💀 Boss Fights (Good Luck)[/h2]  
Boss fights should be some of the best moments in the game.

Instead, you might get:

• Spawned into complete chaos  
• A patch that breaks ascension after the fight  
• A server crash mid-fight  
• Or a random update that kicks everyone out  

Oh, you brought your best dinos and gear?

Poof. Gone.

[hr]

[h2]💰 Monetization Decisions[/h2]  
Want a powerful new dino?

Sure—just be ready to pay for it.

Oh, it’s completely broken and overpowered?  
Don’t worry—they’ll nerf it later… probably.

Mods?  
• Free ones exist (great)  
• Paid ones exist (okay…)  
• Updates will break them anyway (of course)

[hr]

[h2]🔁 Déjà Vu (Again)[/h2]  
Couldn’t get ARK II out?

No problem—just remake the maps from ASE and sell it again.

If you’ve been here before, you’ll feel it immediately.

[hr]

[h2]🧠 Final Verdict[/h2]  
ARK is one of the most frustrating games I’ve ever put thousands of hours into.

It’s broken. It’s inconsistent. It will absolutely test your patience.

…and yet somehow, it still pulls you back in.

[hr]

[b]Final Thought:[/b]  
I complain about this game constantly…  
then log back in to check on my dinos.

After 2 Thousand hours on official my character was deleted due to a bug when transferring servers. I made supports tickets through the proper channels (website,discord) and received no response for almost a month. When my issue was finally adressed everything of mine had been lost. They did not even return the correct character. I am beyond disappointed in the team behind this game. These guys suck.
its a buggy mess that I love and grew up on. replaces your sleep and or job but worth it. dont buy unless you have a good pc

If you love Ark Survival Evolved, you'll likely love Ark Survival Ascended.
once the bugs have been ironed out and the performance is better optimized. 
If you can get past the two glaring gameplay issues, Ark Survival Ascended will eventually become a great remaster of an already great survival game.

What can be said here? ASE was a perfect game. A lot of people could run it without issues, mods were free, and moderators relied on goodwill donations from generous players, especially on the PvE side of things. As for PvP, everything felt solid — maps were released on time, people were having fun, and the community was thriving.

Then Snail stepped in and released this game, forcing players to pay for every necessary DLC just to have a decent experience. They keep pushing new content like drakelings that are completely overpowered in PvP, turning the game into something ASE never was: pay-to-win.

I usually don’t write reviews because I doubt anyone will even see this, but what they’re doing is genuinely unfair. There was nothing wrong with ASE. It was more optimized, and they could’ve easily continued supporting it while releasing new content. Instead, they made this game and later shut down all official ASE servers.

You have to wonder what this game will look like a few years from now. Everything will probably end up locked behind a paywall, all because Snail abandoned its community and focused more on profits than making a functional, enjoyable game for people to play.

They legit wasted thousands of dollars from donators to make animated trailers, hence why they keep releasing new dlcs locked behind a paywall. Yeah, go ahead, and make sense of that.

Cannot recommend this game any more. The release of the latest DLC (rabbit) requires you to buy and own previous DLC's to have it. Forcing you to spend more on a "bundle" than just buying the dlc as it is.

Previous to this, there has been multiple issues regarding meshing, cheating and just overall instability during gameplay. 

I've played a good amount of official ark and unofficial and I can tell you that if it was not for the community that is behind this game, it would not of been made, because it would have died with Ark survival Evolved. 

Official series are actual ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ and wildcard is constantly trying to scrape all the pennies they can get out of your pockets. Conquest this last season had lost all their current dev/admin support down to one guy, which we're all assuming won't be staying for much longer.

Arkpoc is the only thing going with a consistent population month in and month out. But even those servers do not last more than 2 weeks.

PvE Is not much better unless youre on an unofficial server due to the pillar spam every where. Good luck trying to find some where to live.

Alas, the unofficial servers are the only saving grace, and yet I am still not recommending this game, They're just as bad in terms of P2W, cash grabs and gambling.


I hope who ever reads this and still buys the game is playing on their own local or privately rented server with friends, because otherwise it's not worth the time you will invest & the dev's only see you as cashcows.

As an Ark player from console with over 5,000 hours since 2016, I've watched this game evolve tremendously. Unfortunately, despite all that time and development, many of its biggest problems remain unchanged.

At its core, Ark is an incredibly fun game. Whether you play on private servers, PvE, or official PvP like I do, there's a playstyle for everyone. Ark: Survival Ascended has added new creatures, DLC content, and several quality-of-life improvements that genuinely make the game better.

The problem is that these additions also highlight what's wrong with the game: greed, poor priorities, and a shocking lack of technical polish.

I bought Ark on Steam to play with friends, only to discover I couldn't properly interact with certain creatures and structures because I didn't own specific DLC. Instead of offering optional content, the game often feels like it's pressuring players to spend more money just to access features that others in their tribe can use. Some DLC items even provide significant advantages on official PvP servers, making the experience feel increasingly pay-to-win.

My biggest complaint, however, is performance. This has been an issue since the console days and somehow still hasn't been solved. In just 15 hours of playing on PC, I've experienced more crashes than I can count. I'm running a capable system, have spent time troubleshooting, and even play on low settings with optimized INI files. The problem isn't my hardware—it's the game.

Ark remains plagued by crashes, bugs that have existed for years, bloated updates that take forever to patch, and technical issues that seem to be treated as normal by the community. The most recent update reportedly caused some players to lose their characters entirely, wiping out countless hours of progress. Rather than focusing on fixing critical issues, Wildcard continues pushing out new DLC, often in an unfinished state and at an additional cost.

I genuinely wish I could recommend Ark. Few games have created as many memorable moments for me, and the core gameplay is still something special. But when a game struggles to meet basic standards of stability and performance after all these years, I can't endorse it.

Ark is the best terrible game I've ever played.

TL;DR: Great concept, amazing memories, terrible optimization, questionable monetization, and years of unresolved technical issues. Don't buy it expecting a polished experience. Wait for optimization—and then wait a little longer, because it probably isn't coming.

It's not early access, don't let the tag fool you. 
Public servers are infested with cheaters and hackers. Support has been down "for maintenance" for at least a year.
Solo play is kind of boring.
With a group it's fun on a private server, yet again, almost everything is fun with friends. The people make the unfun fun.
Bugs and map errors are plenty to be found.
Basically like the original.
Developers are so greedy. Game uses old assets for many things, and updates require 150+ GB free on storage device for a minor 2-5GB update.

Have been playing for 5 hours now in singleplayer. Every other time I load the game, I hover 100 meters above the game world in an area I haven't been before, about 2000 meters away from my last save. I'm being attacked by alpha raptors while in the air and plummet to my death. This has happened three times already. It's good if you like absurdist comedy. It's trash If you want to play a good game. Go play the old ARK: SE...

I personally love ark, I come from ase and recently switched to asa. I have problems connecting to servers, every time without doubt I must connect, wait to get kicked and connect again. I lose about 15 mins every time I want to join or crash and try to rejoin. On top of that every other time I try to get on there's an update and I got to wait around 1-2 hours for my disc to patch the game. I love this game but it feels like I am waiting more than playing

After a long time, I came back to Ark as many others do. I don't own ASE on pc, but I have played it much on playstation. Now, like anyone with a bad ex that they keep coming back to make out with, I keep coming back to ark because it is a unique and fun game to play with friends, whether or not you want to grub on officials or unofficials, or simply idle in the background and breed or enjoy the aspect of dinos itself.

Now, I do love ASE, but I absolutely cannot support ASA in any official capacity man. I have glitched under the map and died while standing still somehow, glitched off a tame while dismounting a passenger seat, graphical tearing, good textures require me to use all of my VRAM entirely for now reason, leaving me to run it as low as possible because it is unoptimized. Terribly, horribly unoptimized. My pc isn't the best, nor is it the worst either. I am running decent specs at least. But for whatever reason, I have to sacrifice pretty much everything to ensure that it doesn't look like trash sometimes. Now, I know many might not have the same issues, and some will. I am simply here with my case.

Now my pc is very particular about graphical tearing. It usually requires for every game that I have Vsync enabled. I did do that for ASA. Did it do anything? Not a damn thing man. It is absurd to me how it is possible I can even see ♥♥♥♥ when the upper half of the screen glitches when I am looking around, but then suddenly looks clean when I hold it still? Tf do you want me to do? Stand like a statue while I get my ♥♥♥♥ clapped by some random Dilo that spawned on the beach with me? I mean I can bend over for it, but I am not sacrificing all my vram for some high quality even a bit. I can't even put settings on high quality. I can't even put it on medium. I have to set it on low instead and set textures to medium to get anything good. Also why does foliage have so much quality dampening man? I am not an expert on stuff like this, and I might be ranting a little here, but the hell man? ASE is far better optimized than this. I would rather go back to ASE every time than put up with this man. I can play Monster Hunter Wilds on max settings and minimal rtx at best. I can't even play this correctly the way it should. No wonder this game is considered an Early Access game. In my opinion, should've baked it more in the oven before pulling this half baked thing whatever it is out. 19 hours in when I am posting this and I can say I hate it.

Now, I have gotten that chunk off my chest. I am well aware of who is to blame more than anything. To be honest, I don't like Grove Street Games. I have had poor personal experience with them in the past when I played on switch ark. But that is another topic. The real reason this game is ass in optimization, at least I think so, is because of Snail Games. They don't care about this game series. 

The first time I opened up the ASA menu for the game upon first boot up, I was greeted with nothing but pure ♥♥♥♥♥♥ p2w dlc crap. Pyromanes and all other types of crap I don't care about. Man, I am here to wind down and ♥♥♥♥ around and find out against other players or raise dinos and be a groups dedi breeder character. Not pay for your slop. To say I am pissed is an understatement. I just want to play a game man. Go bother EA with how to best get people into the ad economy. They would know how to tell you to best set up your ads. Not to mention the fact you have all these loser ass nerds that immediately pull up with a pyromane and show it off before killing you as some sort of weird cringy flex. Like okay man I get it, you threw your money at a game to be able to tame something that should've already been tamable. Go bother someone else dude.

Also, for a final thing to say, many others like me who can agree, leaving ark permanently is not possible. It is really hard to permanently stop playing this game. We all once in while come back to visit our ex to see how she is doing and if we can tap that. Its toxic, infuriating, and yet for some reason we come back because there aren't many games that are like ark's experience. But, in my case, if I come back to play it will be ASE which was at least better optimized.

I get a little heated when talking about this series, so I am opinionated on it heavily. Critique my pc specs or me, I don't really care man. At the end of the day, it is a game. I am not like some who make this their entire lifestyle or dedicate themselves to it like the cringe megatribes who can't even hold look at a job application without seeing the light of god out of fear. I got better things to do. And that is fine.
is not as good as OG ark alot more paid to win and snail games has basicly gutted it for money
The game runs extremely poorly. i might not have the best gpu but can't even play on medium jezus.

I loved ark as an IP and have played much more than this on Xbox totalling to around 4000 hours however the DLC's introduced are horrific money grabbing slop. 

Do not give any of your money to these developers or publishers as it is clear with a 50% cut on paid mods and a £25 DLC that just straight up gives you an advantage over normal players they are just here for the payday.

I once loved this game and sank countless hours into it, now it is ruined.

I would reccomend it, the game is very nice and visual, but it does require some decently good pc parts and even with good parts its still very heavy, since its very unoptimized, and the most annoying part of it is the ram usage, most games recccomended ram is 16 but this is 32 and 16 is just a baseline, for me i have to play the game on the edge

Imagine opening up a beautiful world of dinosaurs and just playing that game. No mods, no constant need to muck about with the console in order to un-frustrate your player experience. Unfortunately, this is not that game.

Firstly, I'll explain what kind of a gamer I am, because that will colour my review and you may play/feel differently.

I'll sink a lot of hours into a game, and I'll happily grind for resources. But if the game makes grinding miserable, boring, or borderline impossible for a solo person, kills you over and over again before you can achieve anything - I'm going to get annoyed. So, perhaps I'm not the target for a pure survival game. But as this is the only decent dino game that exists, this is the one that everyone plays, and there are just so many things about it that I find frustrating.

I waited until this was under $20 to get it because it has had mostly poor reviews, and we all knew it was a cash grab. It should have been free, and charging exorbitant fees for a basic engine/graphics upgrade was taking the pi$$. The devs had never addressed the performance issues in all the time the game had existed, and the game was buggy as hell. So, it was their responsibility to fix that, and it was beyond audacious to ask us all to pay for them to fix their $hitty product.

So I waited until it was cheap to take a look. The character creation - really disappointing. Most people will spend a fair bit of time on this, but the characters in this game have always been incredibly ugly (especially the men), and your options to change things like hair, eyebrows and facial hair have been severely reduced. God knows why. Perhaps you can collect an anti-ugly token further down the line? Maybe you can pay them to make your character look as if they didn't fall into a vat of boiling lava as a child? I've read on forums you can unlock hair styles, but how, when, who knows? Can I change my face? And alterations to the body too are poor. Why charge us to buy the game again and then make it worse? 

Over the last 24 hours of play I've seen a few minor quality of life improvements, mostly in base building,  like walls you can edit to be door frames of different kinds instead of having to build a bunch of different parts - that's pretty cool. But they don't seem to have improved them beyond that, no half walls in that list of options to make fences that I'm sure used to exist. And I'm already in the console reducing the stupid fog so I can actually SEE, editing the .ini files in order to get things to actually spawn TODAY, not in 6 months time, in order to get the 1500 metal I need to build a furnace, with no creature to help collect because no matter how or when I hunt for them I can't FIND them. I'm turning off the usual irritating lighting effects we all used to have to turn off constantly, and remembering why this game frustrated me so much at the beginning. It just takes so damn long to get past the early frustration.

To me, the game has always felt that there was too big a jump between smithing table recipes and fabricator recipes and that hasn't changed. Everything just feels a bit annoying, a bit underwhelming, a bit ugly. Ah, but we know why. Because then they can CHARGE you for stuff that should be in the basic game. Greedy, greedy, greedy.

And how is this game STILL in early access years after it was released? Is it possibly because they tried to charge full price for a game ported to a newer version of Unreal Engine without making any meaningful changes to said game, and the community said FU, you greedy, lazy, money-grubbing weirdos? Yes. Yes it is. It's an excuse for laziness that's always plagued the devs/publisher's approach to this game. It's as if they resent owning it, but it makes them too much money to kill, so they do the bare minimum.

Let's talk about mods. Mods were Ark's only saviour previously. I would not have played this game without mods. The modding community filled in all the gaps, let you worry about meaningful progress instead of stupid, grindy rubbish, and let you build things that looked a little nicer. Now it seems there's a system of free and 'premium' mods - the Minecraft model, basically, raking in cash, cash, cash, cash. For doing WHAT? The laziness is obscene. There are free mods, but even on their own in-game marketplace, some of these don't seem to work, or ask you to download DLC. Still figuring out what that's about. Without forums, I'd just return this game. And without cheats. If resources don't spawn, I can't collect them, and I can't MAKE anything. Flying or walking around and around and around finding nothing is beyond frustrating and boring. Cheating sucks, I don't want to spawn stuff in, but I've often found that's the only way to get what I need because the game doesn't provide.

The map is still garbage, although it has been improved a little. Zooming in made mine go all pixellated. Bug or a feature? I'm leaning towards 'bug'.  The game feels early access, despite having been released years ago. It's just disappointing. This game has so much time invested in it by its community, but it feels as if they've done all the work, while the devs sat back and pleasured themselves to the frustration. It feels like a game developed by Reddit mods.

So, I don't know. I can't recommend it, unless you get it so cheaply that you don't care if you hate all that extra effort or not. I don't think it's a meaningful upgrade to the previous game which has a healthy modding community who actually cared, and made beautiful models for it and just did such amazing work.

Performance wise it's not putting any strain on my PC on Ultra, but I have a machine built to take the load, so that's not saying much. 

All in all, I doubt I'll end up putting as much time into this as I did into the first game. It's not worth the frustration, and I'm not remotely interested in buying DLC or paying for mods and stupid cosmetics. Sick to death of greedy publishers trying to make their billions with the least possible amount of effort. 

-- All Hail to Hello Games who doesn't do any of that garbage --

I'm sorry but on the steam deck the game is basically unplayable. On steam deck the game is quite literally worse than a PS1 game because a PS1 game is at least playable. This thing has a verified badge when it should say unplayable. 

Furthermore this game 177 GB. You would think that all the DLC would be installed after such a bloated download, but unfortunately only the base map in the island gets installed. So I would assume that if you install more maps, it's only going to bloat the file size even more, while hiding behind the Early Access label. 

Now I have played this game on PlayStation 4, PlayStation 5, in Xbox. The best experience I've ever had with these games is playing them on console. 

Just be aware if you pick this game up that the file size can I necessarily balloon. If your system is older or underpowered, you're going to experience lag, screen tearing, horrible textures, and a ton of pop in. That's on top of the game just being a buggy and glitchy mess. 

On console these games can easily be a 7 or 8 out of 10. Unfortunately on a steam deck this is quite literally one out of 10 game, and it's a shameless cash grab considering the fact that it doesn't actually work properly on the steam deck but it still says verified. It's crazy that steam allows this.
Have played this game for years. Really do love the game, however, I wish they would work on fixing bugs and previous content instead of continuously making new incomplete DLCs.

Long time Ark enjoyer here with well over 1k hrs on the original game on PC and probably just as many on console, had taken a break from ASA and just decided to come back to see what is new. I was sad to find that the only "new thing" is more issues and crappier optimization. I wish wildcard wasn't this bad at caring for their game and even more disinterested in the player base.
Game runs like my arthitic nan, classic example of UE5 slop. still ark tho
if you've been ark'ed before prepare thy anus ascended is quite possibly the the worst version of ark put out ... but at least it looks good

The game was fune for a few days. Then it stopped opening. It launches as a black screen and then crashes shortly later. The second largest waste of money I have ever spent. Do not purchase this game unless you are okay with it possibly not working. I have been trouble shooting for way to long.
This game has no storyline, is harsh on beginners, and is one of the most overrated games.
Even with my setup I cant run this game on stable 60fps. not even with dlss balanced

This game is the most unoptimized piece of trash ever made. Laggy, unresponsive, unfair and not balanced in the slightest. This game is nothing like ark of old and should not have a single positive review. DO NOT DOWNLOAD THIS GAME THE DEVS STEAL YOUR MONEY

i've had to re install this game 2 times because of a 1.8 gb update. the game itself ins super cool the mods ( i tried using them the third download) they are amazing too. but the main game is so buggy and having to re-install constantly has drained me and i barely have 10 hours.
Terribly optimised. Cant play the game above lowest settings, even then i experience constant fps drops
Buggiest game ever, runs like ♥♥♥♥, graphic bugs everywhere.

First off, the game size is just under 300 gb which is a lot but okay, I will just free up some space. Next day there was an update of about 2gb. Apparently having 200gb of free space is not enough and I need at least the same amount of space free as the entire game even for just the smallest update. The only way to bypass this is to fully uninstall and reinstall the game every time they update it. And from the words of other people, they update the game up to three times every week. This is just absurd.

Secondly, we were trying to play on a non-dedicated server but the option to increase teather distance is not present in this game. This means that all players must be close to the host at all times when playing. In ASE there was a setting that could be increased so it didn't matter. It's gone in this game for some reason. Because of this we were researching hosting oppotunities. And holy hell.

This company sold the exclusive rights to sell server hosting to one company. This means that they have a monopole and can charge whatever they want. In ASE (and any other game we have played for that matter) we have been able to get good hosting for around $10 but for this game the absolute cheapest option is $24 per month. This is insanely scummy. I'm guessing they were probably also paid not to have the tether option just so more people would pay for hosting (though I don't know).

Because of these things I can not recommend the game.
To update this 177gb game, you must have 354gb plus the size of the update. Its not like the base game already takes up half your hard drive.

You quite literally need a NASA supercomputer to play this game. The game constantly drops to 0 FPS for around 10 seconds every 2 to 5 minutes. I have a 4K monitor with the specs to play quite literally every game on 4k so far (-this pos game), but I have to play the game at 1920×1080, and even then it still freezes on the lowest settings. Not to mention, it's quite literally the same game as Evolution was, just with more P2W dlcs (skill tree and various engrams only craftable and usable if you spend another 30 (sale btw)) and better graphics that most people can't even enjoy without spending a fortune on a PC. I have tried every possible method to prevent the stutters, yet they still happen. Most recently, my game has started crashing with a GPU dump error if I play for longer than four hours.

and yet sadly the game is still fun because its still the classic ark, but I would rather play evolved if they had some of the nicer "features" they added (which I personally have no clue what they even did)

this is the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game i have ever played. even worse than survival the devs dont do anything about the rampant cheaters and esp users they dont care at all and it ruins the game for everyone else. until the devs put in some actual effort into this problem dont waste money on this garbage game like i did.
Man, what a bad upgrade. The textures are awful, game is not smooth at all. Glad this was 75% off when i got it or I would be mad at money wasted. Definitely going to play the original Ark.
Save your money. ASA is not worth it in its current state. Play something else. this game is not and never will be worth it.

This game is fun but.. They really REALLY need to optimize this game, it's one of the biggest flaws and issues among all the other problems that's never getting fixed. Doesn't help that the DLC maps are humongous as well eating up so much disk space.
Worse than ASE, even worse performance, 100s of uselesss new creatures and even more cheaters on official servers making it basically  unplayable online
objectively just a terrible game, only bought it because ASE is broken and unplayable.  nasa pc required

I regret buying the game even for 15$.

I create a game on extinction which basically come with the base game. Come across a new dinosaure, cannot tame it because I need to buy the DLC to tame it. Continuing my adventure, same patern, new dino, new DLC, only for 1 dino.

I had nothing against a "new map, new dino pack DLC" because it had a lot of content and didint change gameplay on other maps. The thing that triggers me AF is playing on a map I "bought" and not being able to play with everything because I have to microtransation my way to everything I come across.

Gross company.

Do not buy this game, i own this game on PC, XBOX and PS5, every time i get the buzz to play it, but soon as you go back to it, it runs and play like arse, wildcard again have failed to make the game stable, they had the chance to make one of the greatest dino games, but instead of fixing things, they just bring out DLCs that run like arse. if you want to play it, use evolved
I have a pretty good pc and this game runs like crap with medium settings, fav game ruined.

Prime example of quantity over quality. Most of us would gladly wait longer if we knew the next map, dino, etc was of reasonable quality. No one is happy with half baked bread. The game with additions without cosmetics is +$130. That is OBSCENE for a game.

ARK SE has over 5X the reviews and is still rated higher than this game. Honestly, I use to defend this game because I use to think it had potential, now I am just seeing more paid DLC for dinos.

Honestly this game is built of more bugs then it is gameplay and the company behind it shows with every update they couldnt give less of a ♥♥♥♥ about the community and about the game and that all they care about is stealing every bit of your money they can, in short dont buy this cash grab of a buggy mess

Game runs like trash changing settings crashes game

My wife and I loved Ark Survival Evolved. We played it for more than 1400 hours. We decided to try Ark Survival Ascended.

Sorry, guys but in short: don't buy it. Maybe in two or three years' time at a discounted price. Maybe...

We installed it, I spend quite a lot of time tweaking graphics settings but no matter what I tried I couldn't get rid of strange flickering light and shadow effects on Aberration. Yeah, you could say that it works perfectly on other maps (I haven't tried it but honestly I cannot believe that) but come on. I cannot make the game run without annoying graphics flaws on an 5070 RTX? And it is not about the frame rate, it is about playing without annoying problems. Of course it is "early access" so (at least from a legal point of view) this is what you get for your money and shouldn't expect a finished and polished game. 

We struggled with it for two or three days and then went back to Evolved. This was a good opportunity to tell the difference between the two games. My comparison:

Ascended: nicer foliage, (a bit) more colorful
Evolved:: less nice foliage, (a bit) less colorful

That's it. 

For me this difference is not worth struggling with bugs but it is questionable whether in two- or three-years’ time (when the game gets polished) I will not want something more than a bit more colorful and nicer foliage. Apart from that we did not notice any difference and it tells a lot about the game that when I tried to show my wife the difference between the two games and spawned on Evolved, I could run around but when I spawned on Ascended, I spawned inside a rock and couldn't move. 

If you love testing games, reporting bugs in a slightly nicer environment go and buy Ascended.
terrible game horrible optimized i have a high end pc and still get like 10 fps without FG

I underestimated the Bad Reviews. I read a few saying that the game was poorly optimized but they didn't stress this point enough. This is THE MOST HORRENDOUSLY OPTIMIZED GAME I HAVE EVER HAD THE DISPLEASURE OF PLAYING. The very first thing that I had to do when loading up the game is search tutorials for solutions to the terrible graphic glitches that destroy the textures, lighting, and performance. A mixture of fondling the graphic settings and stuffing the console full of commands I did not understand is what ensued. This is not what a gaming experience is supposed to be. After a few hours my game is playable, but certainly not acceptable.

I only wish to emphasize one other major issue. The game is listed as crossplay-crossplatform but this is a LIE. Non-Dedicated servers (which are servers you would run to play with your friends) DO NOT SUPPORT CROSSPLATFORM. PS5, XBOX and PC players can NOT play together on Non-Dedicated servers. If you've played Minecraft Bedrock Edition, you may know that a person creates a world, then others regardless of hardware may simply click and join you. This is NOT how Ark works, but it IS possible. I'm not sure if links can be posted in reviews so I will describe this in text. There is a video on YouTube by the user TroubleChute titled: ARK: Survival Ascended Dedicated Server Setup | Host a FREE private server | Full Guide. This is what a PC player would have to do to create a crossplatform Non-Dedicated server. It is not as simple as the video describes. I ran into many issues not mentioned in the video, but did manage to get it to work after 2 hours.

Other than these 2 major points at the very least this game is what Ark Survival was. At least close enough if you came back for nostalgia. 

Knowing this is the internet I assume I'll have at least some crybaby replies to this review. To them I say that if I can run Ultra Realistic Minecraft and raytraced Wuthering Waves so those thicc buns pop, there is no reason I shouldn't be able to do the same with some crappy dino game remaster. This is just a Cash-Grab.
Game runs like utter trash for what it should be. Devs focus on paid content and creatures and will probably never optimize the game. It's also over 300 GBs. I love Ark, but I can never recommend it.
it's truly a fun game to play, but it has a lot of technical issues. Get it if you have lots of space, but other than that uh, even then it *may* work.

I bought ARK: Survival Ascended because I thought it was “Rust with dinosaurs.”

I was wrong.

This is a prehistoric psychological warfare simulator disguised as a survival game.

I spawned onto a beautiful beach thinking, “Wow, this game looks amazing.”
14 seconds later I was beaten unconscious by a shirtless man named “Tax Fraud” holding a wooden club. 

After recovering emotionally and physically, I decided to live peacefully and become a farmer. The game disagreed.

A dinosaur built like an overgrown turkey broke into my house, killed me, ate my storage boxes, and somehow set my campfire on fire harder.

So naturally I locked in.

I spent the next 9 hours punching trees, collecting rocks, and force-feeding berries to dinosaurs like some kind of unpaid Jurassic Park intern. Eventually I tamed my first raptor and felt unstoppable.

That feeling lasted approximately 3 minutes until a level 140 T-Rex came out of the forest moving at the speed of financial debt and erased my bloodline.

“No problem,” I thought. “I’ll rebuild.”

Big mistake.

ARK players do not sleep.
ARK players do not have jobs.
ARK players hear a single wall placement sound from 4 miles away and immediately launch a full military operation against your 2x2 wooden shack.

I logged off for ONE night and came back to find:

my dinosaurs murdered
my base demolished
my storage emptied
and a sign that simply said “ez”

I have never known hatred like this.

But somehow… I kept playing.

Because every once in a while this game gives you a moment so cool that it completely erases the previous 6 hours of suffering. Flying across the map on a giant bird while your friend gets chased by 19 alligators is genuinely cinema.

Then the game crashes.

Then your dinosaur falls through the map.

Then your generator explodes for reasons unknown to modern science.

Then a seagull steals your narcotics directly out of your inventory while you watch helplessly.

I have over 100 hours now.
I still don’t understand half the mechanics.
I panic every time I hear footsteps in the woods.
My PC sounds like it’s attempting liftoff whenever I open the game.

10/10 experience.
Would absolutely ruin my mental health again.

I really enjoy Ark and was absolutely ecstatic when I had the opportunity to play. My enjoyment quickly became overcome with exhaustion due to the aggressive spec requirements. Going in, I understood that my current setup wouldn't allow max graphics usage, however, with the lowest of lows and an exponentially large majority of textures, clouds and fog disabled I was still unable to run it smoothly. Skip this game entirely until they release some sort of optimization patch and play Survival Evolved instead.
FYI, the base game takes around 170gb, and to update it when a new patch comes out, you need another 170gb of free storage. WTF is this nonsense devs?
So far I love it. Never played it before, or the original, but if you like building, crafting, surviving, dinosaurs and flying - this is a great one!

As an Ark Survival Evolved Fan, I do very much enjoy the quality of life updates that the developers brought to this game. The building mechanic is way better and easier to utilize and the graphics update is lovely. 

That being said, I was really hoping the developers would fix the HORRENDOUS optimization and make it run better and they completely dropped the ball on that. The game is just as glitchy as Survival Evolved, and in some ways more glitchy than Survival Evolved. 

The coding of the dinosaurs is as confused and easily caught up on terrain as ever. They just never get the pathing right and always get stuck on various things. 

You definitely want to play this game with an ethernet internet connection otherwise there will be connection issues as well. 

Overall, if you are the kind of person that loved Ark Survival Evolved you probably will also enjoy this one, but if you're looking for something better than Evolved or don't appreciate bad connection/optimization this probably isn't the game for you. 

As the joke of the Ark community goes: "One does not simply 'Hop' onto Ark". This game is no exception of that. 

I do recommend this game as I am just a fan of Ark, but this game is definitely not for everyone.

I LOVE this game! There is so much content to see and do. All my hours and i still have not left The Island map. It really is that good. I bought it on sale but even at full price its $$ to content value is def worth it. The modding community is huge with many different ways to change up your gameplay. Singleplayer is configurable for your desired gameplay plus there are plenty of servers available to join, both official and hosted by others. PVP and PVE communities.

There are some paid DLC's, I own them all. I am not against purchasing DLC products if it keeps more updates coming. Still recieving awesome updates with soooo much in them. I wanted to want this game again, When i seen it on Sale for Steam i had to grab it. With over 1k hours on ASE and some on ASA on Console and limited on Game Pass PC. I wanted to relive the journey i fell for so long ago cause lets be honest no other game does what this game does. Unfortunately this did not happen 3 days in a row after investing most of my day my single player saves became corrupted loosing all progress i made always around level 70ish. I thought i might have resolved the issue alas to no avail my save was corrupted for the last time last night at about level 70. I was just about to buy all the DLC that i could buy cause i fell for it again luckily i was to lazy to go get my wallet last night so crisis averted. So here we are Negative review, a uninstalled game an a sour feeling about a once beloved game. Buyer Beware this game is great but there is some pain that comes with this game also. Id find something else to play honestly.
